comments_1: Company A's investment bank requested a preliminary meeting and sent a draft non-disclosure agreement.
comments_2: Scheduled meeting was later cancelled because non-management directors were concerned Company A management had not demonstrated involvement.

comments_1: In mid-November 2012, the board authorized management to contact potential financial advisors regarding strategic alternatives, including a possible sale.
Balch Hill amended its Schedule 13D and issued a letter urging cost reductions and exploration of strategic alternatives.
comments_2: Potomac later joined Balch Hill in the activist campaign.

comments_1: Board established a special committee to review strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company.
Company B's president said he intended to present sTec as a possible acquisition target at Company B's management meeting the following week.
Approximately two weeks later, Company B informed sTec that its management was not interested in acquiring sTec and believed it could develop needed technology independently.
Company C expressed potential interest in acquiring particular assets of sTec and said it was considering submitting an acquisition proposal.
In mid-March 2013, Company D's head of corporate development contacted management to express interest in a potential acquisition of the company.
Board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch, decided to confidentially approach likely strategic buyers, and to conduct limited confirmatory outreach to financial buyers.
Legal advisor: Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP. Engaged to provide financial advisory services in connection with strategic alternatives and defensive preparedness.
As directed by the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec.
comments_2: Over the process, BofA Merrill Lynch held discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor.

comments_1: NDA included a 'don't ask, don't waive' provision.
NDA included a 'don't ask, don't waive' provision.
Company C said it was interested only in purchasing limited, select assets of the company, and the special committee decided not to continue discussions.
Among the prospective acquirers contacted after April 1, Company A indicated it was not interested in a potential transaction.
sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.
BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to Western Digital Corporation and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by 05/03/2013.
comments_2: Company G was also sent a process letter on 04/26/2013 with the same deadline.

comments_1: Company D provided a verbal indication of interest at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
comments_2: Company D also detailed additional diligence requirements for submitting a written indication of interest.

comments_1: Company F declined to schedule a management presentation, stating it was interested only in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec.
comments_2: Company F had no further communications with BofA Merrill Lynch or sTec regarding a possible transaction.

comments_1: Shortly after 04/24/2013, Company E indicated it was interested only in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and the special committee decided not to continue discussions.
Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
Written indication of interest to acquire the company.
comments_2: Range of $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash.

comments_1: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.
Initial non-binding indication deadline.
comments_2: Western Digital Corporation submitted an indication of interest; Company D was already seeking an additional week; Company G withdrew.

comments_1: NDA included a 'don't ask, don't waive' provision.
Written non-binding indication of interest.
comments_2: Included a draft exclusivity agreement.

comments_1: Written non-binding indication of interest.
comments_2: Range of $5.00-$5.75 per share in cash.

comments_1: After the board meeting, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to Western Digital Corporation and Company D, requesting responses by 05/28/2013.
Company H said it remained interested in a potential acquisition but was not able to increase its indicated value range.
comments_2: Reaffirmed prior range of $5.00-$5.75 per share.

comments_1: Company H stated on 05/23/2013 that it remained interested but was not able to increase its indicated value range after being told its submitted range was insufficient to move forward in the process.
comments_2: The board later noted that Company H had repeatedly stated it could not increase its price above its prior $5.00-$5.75 per share range.

comments_1: Written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash.
comments_2: Returned a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements.

comments_1: Formal final-round deadline.
comments_2: Western Digital Corporation submitted its response; Company D said it needed approximately two more weeks to submit a written indication and mark-up.

comments_1: After reviewing the 05/28/2013 responses, the board directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request a 'best and final' proposal from Western Digital Corporation and a 'best and final' written proposal from Company D by 05/30/2013.
comments_2: Later that day, BofA Merrill Lynch communicated that request to both bidders.

comments_1: WDC verbally stated that $9.15 per share was its best possible price and reiterated internal concern regarding the price.
comments_2: WDC said it remained focused on announcing a transaction on 06/03/2013.

comments_1: Best-and-final deadline requested on 05/29/2013.
comments_2: Western Digital Corporation confirmed $9.15 per share as its best possible price, while Company D again said it required additional time to submit a written indication.

comments_1: A representative of WDC communicated to BofA Merrill Lynch that WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec and was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time.
comments_2: WDC also discontinued its due diligence efforts with the company at that time.

comments_1: Company D said it would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.
Revised written indication of interest at $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash.
comments_2: WDC said it had seriously considered not proceeding and lowered its price from the prior $9.15 per share indication.
comments_3: WDC said it was otherwise prepared to move forward on the transaction terms previously proposed on 05/28/2013.

comments_1: Written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash.
comments_2: WDC stated that $6.85 per share was its best and final price.
comments_3: WDC said it could be in a position to announce a transaction on 06/24/2013 and otherwise on the transaction terms previously proposed on 05/28/2013.

comments_1: sTec, WDC and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement.
